data.body:
[G3 child-coverage — container review of parent 1f19-adf2-ffb2-4b09 paired with THIS child (S6 1ba5: Docs — private-repo credentials, descriptive errors, ref/source semantics, HMAC trust model); 4-bucket audit against the WHOLE EIGHT-child roster]

VERDICT: EMPTY G3 findings list. The union of the eight children (S1 6d56 materialize core, S2 70c7 cache, S2b b73c janitor, S3 fa49 gate threading, S4 30b3 attested signing, S4b e308 claim-gate ref-coherence/ADR 0002, S5 b7fa CLI/MCP surfaces, S6 1ba5 THIS docs) covers every parent acceptance/success criterion; no uncovered parent criterion is attributable to this docs pairing. Every absence below was cross-checked against the WHOLE roster (parent + all 8 children read whole) before being dismissed; ANTI-FP applied (a criterion covered-by-definition by a named consumer counts).

4-bucket coverage map (parent AC -> covering child/children; THIS docs child's role + bucket):

- AC1 (no code-reading gate reads the server working tree in attested mode; all read a faithful snapshot at the pinned SHA) — FULLY by S1 (6d56 AC1/AC2 faithful read-tree+checkout-index) + S3 (fa49 AC1/AC3 gate re-rooting). THIS child DOCUMENTS the attested/ref semantics (its AC3). Bucket STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this docs child (Scope: "Out: code (S1-S5)"). Not an absence.
- AC2 (signed verdict binds verified_at_sha via the signed MANIFEST CHANNEL — no payload-schema/version change, prior signatures verify — reproducible against that SHA) — FULLY by S4 (30b3 AC1/AC2) atop S1's immutable-SHA basis. THIS child is the DOCS owner of the trust surface: AC3 (signs-vs-never-signs + verified_at_sha recording documented for CLI+MCP) + AC4 (HMAC trust model + limits + in-toto Statement shape), explicitly delegated by S4 (30b3 What: "limits ... DOCUMENTED in S6"). Bucket PARTIALLY (docs half here; binding behavior S4). Not an absence.
- AC3 (local source mode reads the in-place checkout AND cannot produce a signed verdict; source=local is the documented back-out restoring prior in-place read behavior) — by S3 (fa49 AC4 reads in-place + advisory AC "source=local documented as the rollback to in-place reads") + S4 (30b3 AC4 bars signing). THIS child DOCUMENTS attested-signs/local-never-signs + fail-closed-attested/local-still-runs (its AC2/AC3). Bucket PARTIALLY (docs half). The back-out clause has its behavioral/doc home in S3; not an absence.
- AC4 (concurrent gates without git-lock contention/cross-op corruption; cache + janitor reclaim under a defined free-space watermark without disrupting in-flight reads) — FULLY by S1 (atomic population) + S2 (single-flight, delete-on-last-close) + S2b (janitor reclamation under watermark). THIS child DOCUMENTS the disk-cap behavior (its AC5). Bucket STRUCTURAL w.r.t. the mechanism. Not an absence.
- AC5 (private-repo fetch failure surfaces a descriptive, actionable credential error) — SPLIT, with THIS child as the DOCS PRIMARY OWNER: S1 (6d56 AC5) RAISES the actionable error; this child's AC1 (README MCP-setup section + docs/jira-sync-setup.md state the private-repo fetch-credential requirement + how to supply it, grep-able) + AC2 (message + remedy + fail-closed-attested/local-still-runs in a named file) own the documentation; the "actionable"/how-to-supply facet is precisely this child's contribution. Bucket FULLY (raise S1 + docs here). Clean split.
- AC6 (portable across macOS + Linux: no external tar; no hardcoded /tmp; flock+mkdir fallback) — implementation FULLY by S1 (6d56 AC4 + portability seam). THIS child DOCUMENTS REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR (no-hardcoded-/tmp knob) + the EFS/NFS flock caveat (its AC5, xref alto-fruit-punch). Bucket FULLY (impl S1 + docs here). Clean split.
- AC7 (operational tunables — free-space watermark, default ref, grace/age windows — configurable with documented defaults, S2/S3) — implementation by S2b (b73c AC9: watermark/grace/max-age/reverify configurable with documented defaults) + S3 (fa49 ref/source defaults via standard rebar config + REBAR_* precedence). THIS child DOCUMENTS the disk-cap/knobs (its AC5) + origin/main default + ref/source semantics (its AC3). Bucket PARTIALLY (documented-defaults prose facet co-owned; each tunable child carries its own documented-defaults AC). Not an absence.
- AC8 (an ADR captures the architecture + rejected alternatives — PID-lease, payload-schema SHA binding) — authored by S2/S2b (b73c AC10 rejected PID+heartbeat lease + cache/janitor architecture) + S4 (30b3 AC1 manifest-vs-payload SHA-binding decision in the ADR) + S4b (e308 ADR 0002). Bucket STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this PROSE-docs child (the ADR is "authored in S2/S4" per the parent note; Scope "Out: code (S1-S5)"). Both named rejected alternatives have an authoring home. Not an absence.
- AC9 (all EIGHT enumerated child stories closed; make lint + full test suite green) — STRUCTURAL/meta epic-completion criterion; covered by the existence of all eight children (this is the docs story, 1 of 8) + each child's own green-bar obligation. Not an absence at this child.

Success-criteria narrative ("Every code-reading gate verifies a client-specified, immutable, reproducible snapshot at a pinned SHA — never the server's mutable checkout — safely under concurrent distributed use; signed verdicts bind verified_at_sha via the manifest channel (reproducible, no prior-signature breakage); local mode preserves the single-developer verify-before-push workflow") decomposes onto AC1-AC6, all behaviorally owned by S1-S5/S2b. THIS child carries the operator/agent-facing DOCUMENTATION surface the snapshot model newly requires — the new fetch-credential prerequisite, the descriptive failure behavior, the attested-signs/local-never-signs + origin/main + verified_at_sha semantics, the HMAC trust model + limits + in-toto Statement shape, and the REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR/disk-cap/EFS-NFS-flock knobs. It introduces NO behavioral obligation of its own and leaves no clause uncovered.

Named-consumer / delegation cross-checks (covered-by-named-consumer, not absences):
- S4 (30b3) "What"/AC explicitly delegates the HMAC-trust-model + in-toto Statement-shape docs to S6 — claimed here by this child's AC4.
- S1 (6d56 AC5) defers the descriptive credential MESSAGE detail to S6 ("see S6") — claimed here by this child's AC1/AC2.
- S5 (b7fa) scopes prose docs out ("Out: ... prose docs (S6)"); the ref/source PROSE semantics are claimed here by AC3 — a clean schema-text-vs-prose split (S5 owns inline outputSchema/text + documented defaults; this child owns the human prose in named, grep-able files). No double-ownership, no union-coverage gap.
Each cross-ref resolves to a concrete AC on this child; no docs obligation named by a sibling is left unclaimed.

Doc-surface reality check (so each "is documented (named file)" AC is a deterministic grep-able check, not phantom prose): the file_impact targets docs/jira-sync-setup.md and README.md are real; the ADR location (docs/adr/, e.g. 0002-code-drift-invalidation.md per S4b) is consistent with the ADR authored by S2/S4/S4b.

Note (scope, NOT a G3 finding): the standing G4 dependency-gap on siblings S4 (30b3)/S4b (e308) about routing the pinned-SHA snapshot handle into plan_review/attest.py's dependency_hashes()/_rehash()/claim-gate freshness re-check is a CONSISTENCY (G4) code seam between S3/S4/S4b; it does not touch this docs child and leaves no parent criterion unclaimed (AC1/AC2/ref-coherence remain claimed by children).

Conclusion: this child cleanly covers the documentation slice of the parent criteria — it is the docs PRIMARY OWNER of parent AC5 (credential requirement + actionable remedy, grep-able) and AC6 (REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR + EFS/NFS flock portability knobs), and the docs half of AC2/AC3/AC4/AC7. No parent acceptance/success criterion is left uncovered by the eight-story roster. NO G3 finding for this pairing.
